cognito.userpool.usernameconfiguration.C: The `UsernameConfiguration` property type specifies case sensitivity on the username input for the selected sign-in option.

cognito.userpool.usernameconfiguration.C.Description: Specifies whether username case sensitivity will be applied for all users in the user pool through Amazon Cognito APIs.  Valid values include:  +  ** `True` **: Enables case sensitivity for all username input. When this option is set to `True`, users must sign in using the exact capitalization of their given username, such as “UserName”. This is the default value.+  ** `False` **: Enables case insensitivity for all username input. For example, when this option is set to `False`, users can sign in using either "username" or "Username". This option also enables both `preferred_username` and `email` alias to be case insensitive, in addition to the `username` attribute.
cognito.userpool.usernameconfiguration.C.CaseSensitive` <a name=\"cfn-cognito-us:
Enables case sensitivity for all username input\. When this option is set to `True`, users must sign in using the exact capitalization of their given username, such as “UserName”\. This is the default value\.
